title: Immersive Shaderless
body:
# Immersive Shaderless ## Minecraft 1.21.1 | Fabric | 2–4 GB RAM ![Immersive Shaderless by Pollmaha](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/82f88ef87d3f00969ed7264e0e2e4875963d069f.png) Immersive Shaderless is a lightweight, performance-conscious modpack that aims to make Minecraft feel more alive and immersive — without relying on shaders or heavy graphics mods. It’s built around a simple idea: vanilla Minecraft can look and feel great with the right tweaks, without needing 8 GB of RAM and a gaming PC from the future. ### WHat´s the goal here? This pack isn’t trying to reinvent Minecraft. You won’t find dozens of complicated new systems, massive content overhauls, or grind-heavy mechanics. Instead, it’s about refining the base game — making it smoother, more atmospheric, and just generally nicer to play, whether you’re building, exploring, or running a small server with friends. It keeps the vanilla core intact, but wraps it in modern modding conveniences: quality-of-life improvements, performance optimizations, and subtle enhancements to sound, visuals, and world gen. You don’t need to mess with shaders, spend hours tweaking config files, or worry about your framerate tanking in the middle of a forest. ![Key Features](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/cea8e62925d8054e681ba9869b164a405f8729c3.png) ### 🔧 Performance-Focused Runs well on systems with just 2–4 GB RAM allocated. Uses Sodium, Lithium, FerriteCore, and other optimization mods under the hood. Short load times, minimal stutter, and a generally snappy feel — even on integrated graphics. ### 🌍 Immersive, But Subtle Adds dynamic ambient sounds, better weather, smooth camera/animation tweaks, improved lighting, and particle effects — all working together to make the world feel more alive. Nothing is overdone or intrusive — it enhances without distracting. ### 🧰 Quality-of-Life Tweaks Smarter UI, cleaner tooltips, easier inventory management, small tweaks to movement and controls. Crafting, looting, exploring, and building all feel a little smoother and more intuitive — without dumbing anything down. ### 🗺 World Gen & Exploration Vanilla-style world generation with carefully integrated structure and biome mods. Towns, dungeons, and landscapes are more interesting to explore — but still feel like they belong in Minecraft. ### ### 🧑‍🤝‍🧑 Multiplayer-Ready Native support for voice chat, land claiming, controller support, and larger player counts. Stable enough for casual community servers or small friend groups — no special setup required. ![Who this pack is for](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/4df50f6fa0c57eedee2335324b3e95288fcf2441.png) ### If any of this sounds familiar, this pack is probably for you: ### You want Minecraft to look better without using shaders or OptiFine. You’ve got limited RAM or an older system, but still want to play modded Minecraft smoothly. You like the vanilla feel, but wish it had more polish, better sound, and fewer annoyances. You want a modpack that "just works" — no config diving, no crashes, no jank. You’re setting up a lightweight server for friends and don’t want to deal with 200 config folders and 30 overlapping tech trees. ![Technical Details](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ea1ff97c68efcaeef0aea499310c62b5336d53a5.png) Minecraft version: 1.21.1 Modloader: Fabric Java version: 17 or newer Recommended RAM: 2–4 GB Recommended Render Distance: 24 Chunks Multiplayer support: Fully supported, including voice chat and claims Beta Version 0.4.1 and newer contains mid-levels of blood and other things. If you do not like that, play with older versions or remove the mod "Enhanced Visuals" from the modpack once you got a copy.
Example ![An example for blood the mod adds](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/36da1ec0d7ef29f15b056e66bff4ddc122f37017_0.webp)
